policy_statement: Examine opportunities to recover and utilise landfill gases from closed landfills in the Helsinki area, building on past surveys and reports.
mechanism_description: The city will examine options to recover and utilize gas from closed landfills, helping avoid methane releases and potentially recover useful energy.
primary_causal_pathway: Waste disposal (landfill) → landfill gas capture/oxidation/utilization → ↓ CH₄ emissions (± fossil energy displacement) → ↓ net GHG emissions
causal_mechanism_detail:
(1) Change in system/activity: Install/expand operation of landfill gas wells, collection piping, blowers, and control devices at closed (or active) landfills, optionally routing gas to engines/boilers or upgrading to renewable gas.  
(2) Mediating physical process: Methane generated by anaerobic decomposition is intercepted and either combusted (flared or used for energy), converting CH₄ to CO₂ (lower climate impact per unit carbon) and/or producing usable energy.  
(3) Climate-relevant outcome: Reduced net GHG emissions primarily through avoided fugitive methane; secondarily through energy recovery displacing fossil fuel combustion.
dominant_pathway_test: Capturing and controlling landfill gas avoids high-GWP methane emissions that would otherwise be released from the landfill.
mechanism_classification_reasoning:
1) Dominant causal pathway: Landfill gas capture leads to direct reductions in methane emissions, a major mitigation lever in the waste sector.  
2) Why this primary over alternatives: While “energy recovery” can improve resource efficiency and displace fossil energy, the single biggest climate effect across policies using this mechanism is avoided fugitive CH₄ (mitigation), not reduced material throughput (resource efficiency) or hazard reduction (adaptation).  
3) Edge cases applied: If a project only generates energy without materially improving methane capture (rare in this mechanism), benefits could skew toward energy displacement; however the mechanism definition includes methane avoidance via gas recovery/control, keeping mitigation dominant.  
4) Applicability to ALL instances: Any policy that recovers and utilizes landfill gas necessarily targets methane capture/control at the landfill, yielding mitigation benefits regardless of whether energy utilization is implemented.  
5) Note on subtypes: Secondary categorization (e.g., “renewable energy generation,” “waste methane management,” “resource recovery”) is deferred to policy-specific evidence such as whether gas is flared vs. used for electricity/heat or upgraded to RNG.
mechanism_edge_case_notes: If “examine options” results only in monitoring or minor operational changes without capture/control infrastructure, realized mitigation may be limited; classification still reflects the intended dominant mechanism (methane avoidance via landfill gas recovery/utilization).
key_indicators: "Opportunities to recover and utilise the landfill gases"; "will be examined further"; "with the help of surveys and reports made in the past"
co_benefits: Local air quality and odor reduction; potential renewable energy/heat production and energy security
instance_edge_case_notes: Could be interpreted as an information-gathering instrument, but because it is framed as examining opportunities (a decision-support/planning step) rather than disclosure to the public, it fits better as planning-strategy than information-disclosure.
